notes: Body height, standard OMOP concept, confirmed. Range in cm.

Diastolic blood pressure, confirmed. Range in mmHg.

Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 2085-9, confirmed via CDR query (concept_relationship 'Maps to', domain=Measurement, standard=S): n=198,313 persons. Range in mg/dL.

LOINC 8280-0, concept_id 3016258 -- confirmed correct/standard (domain=Measurement, standard=S) but essentially unusable: CDR query found only 22 persons (32 rows) total. AoU's Physical Measurements module apparently doesn't populate this LOINC in practice, despite it being the semantically correct standard concept. NOT viable as a high-completeness phenotype -- recommend dropping waist_circumference/hip_circumference/waist_hip_ratio from the active panel unless a materially different source for waist circumference turns up. Range in cm.

LOINC 8286-7 is CONFIRMED WRONG via CDR query -- maps to concept_id 3002630, 'Thigh - right Maximum circumference', n_rows=0. Candidate replacement LOINC 62409-8 (PhenX - hip circumference protocol) not yet checked -- but given waist_circumference's own confirmed concept has only 22 persons total, hip circumference likely has similarly poor coverage regardless of which LOINC resolves correctly. Not worth spending more query budget on unless waist coverage turns out to be a fluke. Range in cm.

Heart rate, LOINC 8867-4, confirmed via CDR query: n=464,639 persons -- largest cohort of any row here. Range in bpm. In-clinic single-reading pulse, Physical Measurements module -- distinct from fitbit_resting_heart_rate below.
Cholesterol [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 2093-3, confirmed via CDR query: n=204,415 persons. Range in mg/dL.
Creatinine [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 2160-0, confirmed via CDR query: n=285,099 persons. Range in mg/dL.

Hemoglobin [Mass/volume] in Blood, LOINC 718-7, confirmed via CDR query: n=280,701 persons. Range in g/dL. unit_source_value's free-text label is unreliable, even for its own dominant group -- confirmed via query_filter_check.ipynb's hemoglobin appendix: the group literally labeled "g/dL" (261,417 of 280,701 persons) has its own median value of 124, i.e. actually g/L. unit_concept_id is the reliable signal instead -- every sizeable group shares unit_concept_id 8636 ("gram per liter") regardless of what the source-text label says, so pull_phenotype()'s `measurement_unit_normalized` path divides by 10 wherever unit_concept_id = 8636 and leaves unit_concept_id 0/NA (genuinely unknown unit) as reported. Confirmed against the real CDR: drops the implausible-range exclusion rate on this concept from ~90% to ~1% on the full unrestricted cohort.
Leukocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count, LOINC 6690-2, confirmed via CDR query: n=262,563 persons. Range in x10^3/uL.
Platelets [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count, LOINC 777-3, confirmed via CDR query: n=262,037 persons. Range in x10^3/uL.
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 1742-6, confirmed via CDR query: n=243,311 persons. Range in U/L.

DEPRIORITIZED: waist_circumference's confirmed concept has only 22 persons in the CDR -- this phenotype is very unlikely to be usable regardless of hip_circumference's resolution. Not a single measurement -- waist_circumference / hip_circumference for the same person, ideally same visit. concept_id holds both underlying concept_ids (waist,hip, in that order), matching waist_circumference/hip_circumference above once those are confirmed. source == 'derived_ratio' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() -- needs a new code path that pulls both concept_ids, joins on person_id (and nearest measurement_date, not just 'most recent value' independently for each, or the pairing could mix measurements from different visits), and computes the ratio; a materially different shape than every other row here. Body-fat-distribution trait, genetically partly distinct from BMI/overall adiposity -- WHR adjusted for BMI (WHRadjBMI) is the more standard GWAS phenotype in the literature (GIANT consortium) and shows notably stronger genetic signal in women than men; adjusting for BMI isn't implemented here (would mean using bmi as a regression covariate for another phenotype, not currently how pull_covariates()/build_covariate_sets() work) but is worth keeping in mind if this trait's results look unexpectedly weak or sex-inconsistent.

AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Concise), sum of 3 items each scored 0-4 (frequency, typical quantity, 6+-drinks/binge occurrence). concept_id holds the 3 underlying question concept_ids (comma-separated), confirmed via CDR query -- the PPI vocabulary's own concept_ids for the frequency/quantity items (Alcohol_DrinkFrequencyPastYear/Alcohol_AverageDailyDrinkCount) are non-standard and entirely unpopulated in observation_concept_id; these are what they "Maps to" instead (concept_relationship, same pattern as hip_circumference's LOINC bug above). Answers are value_as_concept_id-coded, not plain numbers -- each item's answer options were verified to map cleanly onto the standard 0-4 AUDIT-C scale; the score lookup lives in residualize_phenotypes.ipynb's "Derived phenotype: alcohol AUDIT-C score" cell, not pull_phenotype() (same reason as waist_hip_ratio -- doesn't fit its one-concept-id-in/one-value-out shape). Composite is only defined when all 3 items are answered; PMI:Skip/Don't-know responses on any item leave the total NA.

Cigarettes smoked per day, from AoU's Lifestyle survey (Tobacco Use Supplement to the Current Population Survey items). concept_id holds 2 comma-separated PPI concept_ids -- current-smoker daily count (40766929) and lifetime-average daily count (40766930); AoU branches by current-vs-former smoking status, so a person answers at most one. Both confirmed real/standard/populated via CDR query, with clean value_as_number answers (Don't-know/Skip/prefer-not-to-answer responses are value_as_concept_id-coded instead, already excluded by pull_phenotype()'s value_as_number IS NOT NULL filter -- no score lookup needed, unlike alcohol_audit_c_score above). source == 'survey' is wired up in pull_phenotype() -- same most-recent-answer-per-person shape as 'measurement', against {CDR}.observation instead, which naturally coalesces across both branch concept_ids via the IN (...) + ROW_NUMBER() logic.
